name: form
title: Form
description: A real, progressively enhanced <form> built on Base UI's Form that coordinates multi-field validation and routes server-side errors. Keeps native action/method/onSubmit semantics (works without JS), consolidates errors keyed by field name, surfaces each to the matching Field's error message and aria-invalid via useFormFieldError, and moves focus to the first errored control on a failed submit. Compose a page-level error summary from useFormErrors.
useWhen: Coordinating several Fields and needing server-side errors routed back to the right controls after submit
Wanting a real form that submits with action/method and still works without JavaScript, with client and server validation reconciled
Managing focus to the first error (or an error summary) when a submit comes back invalid
avoidWhen: A single field — the field component alone carries its label, hint, error live region, and aria-invalid
Needing a full client-side form-state library (validation schemas, arrays, wizard state) — Form only handles error-routing and submission wiring
A control that triggers an immediate action rather than submitting values (use a button)

keyboard: Tab / Shift+Tab move between the form's controls in document order
Enter submits from a text control; the submit button is reachable and activates with Enter or Space
On a submit that returns errors, focus moves to the first errored control in document order
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default is met by the fields and buttons composed inside the form
notes: Progressive enhancement: renders a native <form> with standard action/method/onSubmit, so submission and server validation work without JavaScript; server errors return via the errors prop.

Server errors are keyed by control name and routed to the matching field, which announces them in a polite live region and sets aria-invalid (WCAG 3.3.1). Pair each message with a correction suggestion for 3.3.3.

Focus is moved to the first errored control on a failed submit; for longer forms set focusOnError={false} and move focus to a page-level error summary composed from useFormErrors() (e.g. the validation component).
The initial mount never steals focus even when server errors are already present — such pages should lead with an error summary.
